page: Amazon Web Services i8ge.metal-24xl is a storage-optimized bare-metal server powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 96 dedicated physical cores built on the 64-bit ARMv9 architecture using the Neoverse-V2 model, operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The system is equipped with 768 GB of DDR5 memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 60,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 150 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance across multiple categories. The multi-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, ranks in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with both cached small-block read operations and uncached large-block read/write operations placing in the top 10%. The server also demonstrates top-tier capabilities in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput. However, single-core performance and small-model text generation tasks show lower relative efficiency.

Given its massive local storage capacity and high memory-to-core ratio, this server offers high resource density for data-intensive deployments. It is highly suitable for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, data decompression pipelines, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads that benefit from dedicated ARM64 hardware without virtualization overhead.
description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.metal-24xl is a storage-optimized bare-metal server featuring 96 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The platform is equipped with 768 GB of DDR5 memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside 60,000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage. It supports a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps. Performance benchmarks place this server in the top tier for multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations, with exceptional throughput in Redis SET operations and static web serving. This hardware profile offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive deployments. It is designed for high-throughput, data-intensive workloads such as large-scale databases, caching layers, and enterprise data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.metal-24xl is a storage-optimized bare-metal server featuring 96 ARMv9 cores, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 60,000 GB local NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput database workloads.

tagline: A high-capacity bare-metal server featuring ninety-six ARMv9 cores and sixty terabytes of local NVMe storage for intensive database workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 physical cores at 2.8 GHz
768 GB of high-speed DDR5 system memory
60,000 GB of high-performance local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU and database operation benchmarks
Optimized for large-scale databases, Redis, and high-throughput web serving
